core_mechanism: consistent matching of a truncated exact low-order perturbative correction with an all-orders iterated stochastic multiplicative branching cascade (a Markov emission process weighted by no-branching survival probabilities), generating the hardest step from the exact term and softer steps from the resummed cascade while subtracting their overlap to avoid double-counting
brief_reason: The abstract describes interfacing a next-to-leading-order (exact low-order) calculation with a shower Monte Carlo (an iterated stochastic branching cascade) via the POWHEG method, and benchmarking it against resummed and higher-order results, which is exactly a fixed-order-plus-resummed-cascade matching scheme.
